question: What did Stellar's June 2026 Quantum Preparedness Plan commit to, and which quantum-safe features were still roadmap items?

golden.answer: SDF published the Quantum Preparedness Plan on June 9, 2026 as a three-stage roadmap, not an announcement that Stellar was already quantum-safe. Stage 1 targets ML-DSA-44 and ML-DSA-65 signature-verification host functions for Soroban during 2026 so contract accounts can adopt quantum-safe authentication. Stage 2 targets protocol-level opt-in migration in 2027, and the plan says every Stellar account is expected to be able to add a quantum-safe signer by the end of 2027. The article explicitly says the quantum-safe signer type and verification primitives were missing at publication, so dates and capabilities are plans/expectations rather than shipped guarantees.
keyFacts: SDF published the Quantum Preparedness Plan on June 9, 2026.
Stage 1 plans ML-DSA-44/65 verification host functions for Soroban contract accounts during 2026.
The plan is a roadmap; it did not mean Stellar accounts were already quantum-safe by default.
avoid: Do NOT claim ML-DSA host functions or a native quantum-safe signer had already shipped when the plan was published.
Do NOT turn the 2026/2027 targets into guaranteed completion dates.
golden.notes: Roadmap status is volatile and must be reverified. Grade the dated distinction between planned and shipped behavior.

corroboration.claim: SDF published the three-stage Quantum Preparedness Plan on 2026-06-09

evidence.note: Primary page embeds date 2026-06-09 and describes the stages.
General-web sweep corroborates the dated SDF announcement and found no earlier shipped-feature claim.

corroboration.claim: ML-DSA-44/65 host functions and a quantum-safe signer were planned rather than shipped as of 2026-07-11

evidence.note: Article says the signer type and verification primitives are the piece missing today and places them on the roadmap.
Live Mainnet reported Protocol 27; checked against the official protocol-upgrade inventory, which did not list ML-DSA host functions as activated.
